solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int lengthAfterTransformations(string s, int t, vector<int>& nums) {
        const int MOD = 1e9 + 7;
        vector<long long> count(26, 0);
        for (char c : s) {
            count[c - 'a']++;
        }

        for (int step = 0; step < t; step++) {
            vector<long long> nextCount(26, 0);
            for (int i = 0; i < 26; i++) {
                int move = nums[i];
                for (int j = 0; j <= move; j++) {
                    int nextChar = (i + j) % 26;
                    nextCount[nextChar] = (nextCount[nextChar] + count[i]) % MOD;
                }
            }
            count = nextCount;
        }

        long long result = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 26; i++) {
            result = (result + count[i]) % MOD;
        }

        return result;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(t * 26)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: The solution uses a dynamic programming approach to track the count of each character after each transformation. Instead of simulating the string directly, it simulates the transformation of character counts. For each transformation, it distributes the count of each character to the next characters based on the `nums` array. This avoids the need to process the entire string repeatedly, making the solution efficient for large inputs. The time complexity is linear in the number of transformations and constant in the alphabet size.
